title: if any of the quick log (important ones only) has not been used for a while i...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

if any of the quick log (important ones only) has not been used for a while it slowly changes the color to a warning color. For example no tummy for 3 days etc. no Breast R for last 12 hours etc. shouldn’t happen for others. Review the best guidelines from the relevant sources & then only. You can add subtle emoji addition or change etc for these cases. Should be very professional

---

**Category:** `enhancement` `ui` `feeding`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
User wants important quick-log buttons to visually warn when they haven't been used for a while.
- Affected area: Quick log buttons/grid in the main dashboard UI.
- Current behaviour: Quick log buttons have static colors regardless of how recently they were last used.
- Expected behaviour: Important quick-log items (e.g., tummy time, breast feeding L/R, diaper) should gradually change to a warning color (e.g., amber/red) if not logged for a configurable period (e.g., no tummy time for 3 days, no breast R for too long).
- Edge cases: Only "important" logs should have this behavior (not all); thresholds may vary per log type; color transition should be gradual/smooth; need to determine which logs are "important" and what the time thresholds are; reset the color when the item is logged.
- Acceptance criteria: Important quick-log buttons change color based on time since last use, with sensible per-type thresholds. Non-important buttons remain unchanged.
implementation_notes:
Added warning color system for important quick-log buttons based on time since last use.
- Files changed: src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx
- Added useMemo hook (quickLogWarnings) that computes warning levels for important buttons
- Important buttons with thresholds: Breast L/R (warn 4h, danger 6h), Tummy (warn 48h, danger 72h), Wet diaper (warn 6h, danger 10h), Dirty diaper (warn 24h, danger 48h)
- Two warning levels: amber tint (warn) and red tint (danger) applied to background, border, and label color
- Warning colors only apply when the button is not active/highlighted (active timer or sleep state takes priority)
- If no log exists at all for an important type and birth is set, shows danger level
- Non-important buttons (Formula, Breast Milk, Sleep) are unaffected
- Trade-offs: Thresholds are hardcoded based on common pediatric recommendations; could be made configurable later
